senses.gloss: guilty, delinquent, criminal, flagrant, vicious, evil
perpetrator, offender, delinquent, criminal, culprit, felon, evildoer
Jānn, the forefather of the race of jinn

examples.text: وقد كان فيها قبل أن يخلق بألفي عام الجن بنو الجان، ففسدوا في الأرض.
examples.english: Two thousand years before [Adam] He [God] created the jinn, the sons of Jann, on earth, and they spread corruption and shed blood therein.

senses.gloss: a jinn; a genie, a jann.

examples.text: لَمْ يَطْمِثْهُنَّ إِنْسٌ قَبْلَهُمْ وَلَا جَانٌّ
examples.english: touched them has not mankind before them nor a jinn.

senses.gloss: a serpentine or draconic class of the jinn
any supernatural being in general, especially elves and fairies.

etymology.text:
Derived from the active participle of جَنَى (janā, “to gather, to harvest; to commit a crime”).

Has the form derived from the active participle, as if from جَنَّ (janna, “to hide”) (in the passive جُنَّ (junna, “to go crazy”)).
